page.content: The table presents diagnostic metrics for various anthropometric measurements in men and women. For men, the Body mass index (kg/m2) has an AUC of 0.676, a SE of 0.008, a 95% CI of 0.660 - 0.691, an optimal cutoff of 28.7, a sensitivity of 66.7%, and a specificity of 59.9%.

page.content: The Waist circumference (cm) shows an AUC of 0.672, a SE of 0.008, a 95% CI of 0.657 - 0.687, with an optimal cutoff of 99.4, a sensitivity of 69.5%, and a specificity of 55.8%. The Waist-to-hip ratio yields an AUC of 0.646, a SE of 0.008, a 95% CI of 0.631 - 0.661, an optimal cutoff of 0.95, a sensitivity of 68.9%, and a specificity of 53.2%. The Waist-to-height ratio has an AUC of 0.687, a SE of 0.008, a 95% CI of 0.673 - 0.702, an optimal cutoff of 0.60, a sensitivity of 66.1%, and a specificity of 61.2%. For women, the Body mass index (kg/m2) presents an AUC of 0.759, a SE of 0.006, a 95% CI of 0.746 - 0.771, an optimal cutoff of 29.2, a sensitivity of 71.8%, and a specificity of 66.9%. The Waist circumference (cm) shows an AUC of 0.773, a SE of 0.006, a 95% CI of 0.760 - 0.785, an optimal cutoff of 90.4, a sensitivity of 74.5%, and a specificity of 67.6%. The Waist-to-hip ratio has an AUC of 0.722, a SE of 0.007, a 95% CI of 0.708 - 0.735, an optimal cutoff of 0.84, a sensitivity of 71.0%, and a specificity of 61.9%. Lastly, the Waist-to-height ratio has an AUC of 0.776, a SE of 0.006, a 95% CI of 0.764 - 0.788, an optimal cutoff of 0.58, a sensitivity of 76.6%, and a specificity of 65.6%.
